step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: The segmentation network architecture separates density maps that allow each pipeline to model its respective macromolecule in the DeepTracer-2.0 pipeline.

Mostosi P, Schindelin H, Kollmannsberger P, et al. Haruspex: a neural network for the automatic identification of oligonucleotides and protein secondary structure in Cryo-electron microscopy maps. Angew Chem Int Ed Engl 2020;59:14788–95.

issues.reason: Text explicitly attributes 40³ voxels input to Haruspex, not DeepTracer-2.0 segmentation network directly, though implied by context.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Clarify that segmentation architecture is based on or similar to Haruspex, or remove specific voxel dimensions if not explicitly stated for DeepTracer-2.0.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: normalize the density value of each voxel from a range of 0 to 1 and resize each map into a 64 [3] voxel size.

issues.reason: Step lacks citations despite claims being supported by text.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add reference to the section describing preprocessing (e.g., [p3] text).

evidence.quote: The initial amino acid network was trained with 1800 experimental maps and their corresponding deposited model structures [5].
the amino acid U-Net determines the positions of the Cα and other backbone atoms
For the nucleotide network training, 293 EMDB/PDB pairs were selected... resolution was 4 Å or better... one nucleotide chain that is at least 20 base pairs or larger
The atoms for the nucleotide UNet focus on whether each voxel contains a phosphate atom (P), a carbon one atom (C1'), a carbon four atom (C4') or no atom. This has a total of four channel outputs.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: The backbone U-Net determines if each voxel is part of the sugar phosphate backbone, part of the nitrogenous base or not in either group. This has three different channel outputs.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add reference to the section describing Nucleic acid network architecture.

evidence.quote: postprocessing model allows phosphate atoms that are within 8 Å from each other.
Most DNA and RNA conformations fall within the 5.9 Å range
The Brickworx model then uses the P atoms and Cryo-EM map to finish modeling the nucleotide.

step_scores.label: partially_supported

evidence.quote: each macromolecular type requires corresponding steps to connect the backbone atoms correctly.

issues.claim: matching_threshold: Cα position within 3 Å of model
issues.reason: Text defines 3 Å threshold under 'Evaluation metrics' for matching percentage calculation, not as a postprocessing parameter for chain connection.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Remove 3 Å threshold from parameters or clarify it is an evaluation metric, not a processing parameter. Add references.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: When both predictions finish, the protein and DNA/RNA structures can be combined to give a final prediction of the macromolecular model

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add reference to Figure 3 description or pipeline overview section.

nucleotide chains are at least 10 nucleic acids or larger.
